subject: Anthropology
title: Disability and Culture
description: A short lesson to help you apply Disability and Culture.

hook.text: A deaf person in Martha's Vineyard wasn't disabled — everyone there signed. Disability depends on context.

buildup.text: The social model of disability argues that people are disabled by barriers in society, not by their bodies alone.

discovery.text: Cultures define disability differently. What's disabling in one society may be normal or even valued in another.

twist.text: The medical model treats disability as individual defect. The social model reveals it as a product of excluding design.

climax.text: Disability is cultural, not just medical. Changing the environment changes who is disabled and who isn't.

punchline.text: Society disables people more than bodies do.

quiz.question: What does the social model of disability argue?
options: Disability is purely a medical condition
People are disabled by social barriers, not just their bodies
